# The & metarule pattern matches anything except / and .
# Unlike %, it restricts to simple names (no path separators or dots)
mk -n -p 1 -f mkfile foo.o
cmp stdout expected

-- mkfile --
&.o: &.c
	cc -o $target $stem.c
foo.c:
	generate foo.c
-- expected --
foo.c: generate foo.c
foo.o: cc -o foo.o foo.c
